2026-05-04 Reuters yen intervention fears Japan shut for Golden Week holidays

2026-05-04 日本市場 休場 みどりの日 JPX

snapshot: Japan's cash equity market is closed for Greenery Day. JPX's holiday calendar lists May 4, 2026 as Greenery Day.
At 12:18 JST, Nikkei 225 futures were 59,900, up 480. Holiday derivatives are trading, but there is no cash-equity price discovery.
Reuters reported that while Japan was closed, MSCI Asia-Pacific ex-Japan rose 0.6%, Nikkei futures were 59,630, Brent was $108.30 and U.S. crude was $102.01.
USD/JPY was 156.94 in the Reuters global-markets story and 156.885 in a separate Reuters FX story. The yen is slightly firmer after suspected intervention last week.
Buyback announcements from M3, DTS and Alinco after the May 1 close remain unprocessed in cash trading and will roll into the May 7 session.

drivers.driver: No same-day cash watchlist because Japan is closed
drivers.why_today: Japan cash equities are shut for Greenery Day, so there is no live order book, turnover, sector-relative action or opening reaction to validate a midday long ranking.

drivers.driver: Middle East, oil and AI-capex concerns dominate the external setup
drivers.why_today: Reuters highlighted the Strait of Hormuz shipping issue, Brent above $108, rising AI capex, and a more hawkish central-bank backdrop. These inputs can still reset the May 7 cash open.

drivers.driver: Buyback catalysts are being deferred to the post-holiday cash session
drivers.why_today: M3, DTS, Alinco and Kyocera have shareholder-return catalysts, but the holiday means there is no same-day cash-market reaction. The first real check is the next cash session.

headline_inventory.headline: JPX's holiday calendar lists May 4, 2026 as Greenery Day, meaning Japan's domestic cash market is closed
Reuters reported modest gains in Asian shares while Japan was closed, with Brent at $108.30 and U.S. crude at $102.01
Reuters reported USD/JPY at 156.94 in thin holiday conditions after suspected intervention last week
Reuters' FX story showed USD/JPY at 156.885, with the yen slightly firmer after suspected yen-buying intervention
Nikkei 225 futures were 59,900 at 12:18 JST, up 480, with the trading date shown as May 7
M3 announced a buyback capped at 3.00% of shares outstanding and 20 billion yen
Alinco announced a buyback capped at 5.5% of shares outstanding and 1 billion yen
DTS announced a buyback capped at 3.08% of shares outstanding and 5 billion yen, with acquired shares to be fully cancelled
Sanko Sangyo announced cancellation of treasury stock equal to 1.10% of shares outstanding
Kyocera announced a buyback capped at 11.88% of shares outstanding and 250 billion yen, plus a cancellation equal to 6.05% of shares outstanding
Nippon Gas announced cancellation of treasury shares equal to 4.3% of shares outstanding

deep_dive.title: Greenery Day holiday edition: no watchlist today, only a carry-over map into May 7

lines: May 4, 2026 is Greenery Day and Japan's cash market is closed, so there is no live order book, turnover or sector-relative tape to validate stock selection.
For this midday edition, the long watchlist is intentionally empty and the focus is on external inputs plus unprocessed after-close disclosures for the May 7 cash reopen.
JPX's calendar confirms the May 4, May 5 and May 6 market holidays, leaving Japan without cash-equity price discovery through Golden Week.
Reuters still reported Brent above $108, USD/JPY in the upper 156s, Nikkei futures at 59,630 and concerns about AI capex, so the setup can still change while cash is closed.
Into the May 7 open, the first question is whether Nikkei 225 futures around 59,900, USD/JPY in the upper 156s and Brent around $108 remain stable or reprice.
The second is whether buybacks from M3, DTS, Alinco and Kyocera can become stock-specific support once cash trading resumes.
Middle East headlines, U.S. payroll expectations or major earnings could materially change futures, FX and oil assumptions while Japan is closed.
Holiday futures and FX moves should not be mapped mechanically onto individual cash equities without fresh price discovery.
A holiday edition has no same-day cash price discovery, so ranking precision is intentionally reduced.
Additional disclosures or overseas events before May 7 can change the priority of May 1 after-close materials.

no_trade_conditions: When the domestic cash market is closed and there is no same-day price discovery to validate stock selection
When holiday moves in futures, FX or crude are being copied mechanically into individual cash-equity ideas
When Middle East headlines, U.S. payroll expectations or major earnings materially alter the May 7 premise
When May 1 after-close TDnet filings are altered in substance by follow-up disclosure or company clarification before May 7
